title: Analytical Applications of 1-Amidino-2-thiourea. Part IV. Gravimetric Determination of Mercury(II)
author: B. A. Nadkarni
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A new gravimetric method for the determination of mercury (II) has been developed, based on the quantita­tive precipitation of INS from ammoniacal solution of mercury salts by 1-amidino-2-thiourea (ATU) Small amounts (16.0 mg.) of mercury can be estimated with an accuraey \(\pm\) 0.2%. Addition of a suitable complexing agent suppreases the interference by other cations. None of the anions interferes. Under the experimental conditions employed, 1-amidino-2-thiourea behaves as a selective reagent for determination of mercury.

Under the experimental conditions employed, 1-amidino-2-thiourea behaves as a selective reagent for determination of mercury.
